sources.title: Release pipeline gate — v0.3.0 (voice TTS + voice commands)
Build doctor pre-v0.3.0 — debug build clean, native DLLs pinned
Security gate pre-v0.3.0 — voice subsystem 1-commit delta audit
Release pipeline gate — v0.2.1 (terminal diagnostics + UX docking)
Build doctor pre-v0.2.1 — 8-commit delta, no DLL drift

tried: Ran two back-to-back release-build-pipeline gates (v0.2.1 UI/dock, then v0.3.0 voice feature) under the same security-guard → build-doctor cadence
Shipped voice subsystem v0.3.0 — AI-mode TTS auto-speak on every bot bubble, VoiceCommandInterceptor ("그만" stop / terminal-summary / pass-through), Whisper hallucination filter
Hardened AgentToolLoop.ParseToolCall (TryGetProperty over GetProperty) so malformed JSON from non-GBNF backends funnels through the controlled JsonException path
Embedded ITerminalSession.GetConsoleText() snapshots into AIMODE prompts via triple-backtick fences for the new terminal-summary voice command
Widened cloud TTS provider exfil from "final bubble only" to "every bubble" gated by user-selected TtsProvider
solved: Two consecutive release gates cleared with zero Critical/High findings — the delta-audit pattern (small diff + 7-scope sweep + carry-forward labelling) is reproducible
VoiceCommandInterceptor proven privilege-neutral: StopSpeaking is local audio teardown, PassThrough reuses the existing v0.2.0 ConPTY route, SummarizeTerminal stays inside the AIMODE consent envelope
Native DLL pinning, version pipeline, IPC payload, and EF migrations verified unchanged across both releases — csproj diff empty in v0.2.1 (8 commits) and v0.3.0 (1 commit)
remaining: BuildTerminalSummaryPrompt fence-injection (Low / CWE-77 / OWASP LLM01) — fence-escape, length cap, and system-prompt clause deferred to the next release cycle
Cloud TTS provider settings lack a per-bubble exfil warning UI (Info, new in v0.3.0) — explicit "speak final reply only" toggle would let power users bound the surface
Voice transcript shell-escape (Medium, carry from v0.2.0) and GGML download hash verification (Low, library-internal) remain open across three consecutive releases

learned.lead: Indirect prompt injection becomes the dominant new risk class once an agent can read its own UI surfaces.
learned.body: The v0.3.0 voice delta added zero shell-exec primitives, zero new packages, zero new actor paths — the only new attack surface was a single LLM prompt that fences user-visible terminal output. That is now the entire delta-relevant security class for this kind of feature. Future audits should pre-emptively scan for GetConsoleText(), Clipboard.GetText(), screen-capture, or any "summarize what the user is seeing" feeder paths and treat them as fence-injection candidates by default. Next action: extract a shared fence-escape + length-cap utility into ZeroCommon so every screen→LLM-prompt site routes through one hardened helper instead of inlining the fence.
